(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER JOEL WARNE'S Motion For: Sanctions Pursuant To, Inter Alia, C.C.P. 128.7(C)(1) And Civ. Code 3294 In Re: False Representations; Reconsideration Pursuant To, Inter Alia, C.C.P. 1008, Et Seq.; Declaratory Judgment Clarifying Scope Of Orders.

Petitioner Joel Warne's motion for sanctions and reconsideration of the order dated May 10, 2017 is denied in its entirety. As to the portion of the motion seeking sanctions, Mr. Warne fails to show that CCSF engaged in conduct that is frivolous, improper, or "indisputably without merit." Mr. Warne complains that CCSF failed to produce his "Discharge Summary." CCSF more accurately refers to this document as discharge instructions. Mr. Warne never signed the document. CCSF explains that when a patient does not sign the instructions, they do not become part of a patient's medical records. (O'Donnell Dec., pars. 6-9.) Therefore, there is insufficient evidence to show that Mr. Warne did not receive a complete set of his medical records. For the same reason, there is no basis to reconsider the May 10, 2017 order and the request to do so is untimely per CCP 1008. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
